get.description:
Gets the available permissions within the scope of a resource. These permissions can be applied to roles and are used to determine user/robot access to resources.

Requires permission: `sanity.{resourceType}.roles.read`

post.description:
Creates a custom permission on a resource.

Requires permission: `sanity.{resourceType}.roles.create`
Requires feature: `advancedRolesManagement`

See Example. It creates a permission to allow a user to read legal documents in project `c7ja4siy` and dataset `production`.

delete.summary: Remove own user from a resource.
delete.description: This removes the currently authenticated user from the given resource. If the resourceType is `organization`, this will also remove the user from all projects owned by the organization. This is a specific route which does not require resource permissions, since users should be able to remove themselves from resources without requiring an administrator to intervene for them.

put.summary: Replace the role set for a user.
put.description:
Replace the role set for a user on a resource. The supplied `roleNames` become the user's complete role set: missing roles are added, extra roles are removed. The list must contain at least one role: at least one role must remain assigned at all times. Roles are assigned before extras are revoked so the user never momentarily drops to zero roles.
A single composite `members.role.replace` activity event is emitted per call (no per-role events).
Requires permission
  - `sanity.{resourceType}.members.update`

  - `sanity.{resourceType}.members.update`

delete.summary: Remove a role from a user in a resource.
delete.description:
You cannot remove the last role from a user. You must have at least one role assigned to a user. Requires permission
  - `sanity.{resourceType}.members.update`

get.summary: Get current user's invites filtered by status/resource
get.description: Retrieves a list of invites for the current user, using the email.

Retrieves a list of invites for the specified resource.

Only pending invites are retrieved by default. Use the optional `status`
parameter to change the filter behavior. You can select multiple
statuses by repeating the parameter.

### Children invites

By default, only invites for the specified resource are returned. Use the
optional `includeChildren` parameter to include invites for children
resources as well. This only applies to `organization` resources.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.members.read`

post.description:
Creates an invite for the specified resource.

The invitee will receive an email with a link to accept the invite.

Each invitee can only receive one invite per resource and role.
Attempting to create an invite using a non-existent role, or a role that
cannot be granted to users will result in a Bad Request error.

### Unavailable resources

If the underlying resource is unavailable then a Bad Request error will
be returned. A common example of an unavailable resource is a project
that is blocked or archived.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.members.invite`

Additionally, only administrators can invite other administrators.

delete.description:
Revokes an invite.

Attempting to revoke an invite that has already been accepted or revoked
will result in a Bad Request error.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.members.invite`

get.description:
Retrieves an invite using its token.

### Authorization

This endpoint does not require authentication.

post.description:
Accepts an invite using its token.

Attempting to accept an invite that is already accepted or revoked will
result in a Bad Request error.

Once the invite has been accepted, the user will have access to the
resource with the role assigned as part of the invitation.

> **Access is propagated internally and may take a up to a few minutes to
> be fully available across all systems.**

### Unavailable resources

If the underlying resource is unavailable then a Bad Request error will
be returned. A common example of an unavailable resource is a project
that is blocked or archived.

### Member quota

Some resources have a limit on the number of members. If accepting an
invite would go over this limit, then a Payment Required error is
returned.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session.

402.description: Resource reached its allowed number of members.

get.summary: Get robots with access to this resource
get.description:
Retrieves a list of robots that have access to the specified resource.

### Access to children resources

By default, this endpoint returns robots that have at least one role on
the specified resource. Use the optional `includeChildren` parameter to
include robots that have a role on children resources as well. This
only applies to `organization` resources.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.tokens.read`

post.description:
Creates a robot with the specified role grants and returns its secret
token.

Only the specified resource and its children resources can be specified
in the role grants. The robot will not have access to other resources.

### Secret token

The secret token is only returned once and can not be retrieved again.
If the token is lost, a new robot must be created. The previous robot
should be deleted.

### Email notifications

For project robots, an email notification is sent to all project administrators
by default when a robot is created. This can be disabled by setting
`sendNotification=false`, but only for projects created within the last 5 minutes.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.tokens.create`

parameters.name: sendNotification
parameters.description:
Whether to send email notification to administrators when creating project robots.
Only applies to project resource types. Can be set to false for projects created within the last 5 minutes.

get.description:
Retrieves a robot using its unique identifier.

It's not possible to retrieve a robot token.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.tokens.read`

put.description:
Updates the expiry date for a robot token.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.tokens.create`

expiresAt.description: When the robot token should expire.

404.description: Resource, robot, or token not found.

delete.description:
Deletes a robot and revokes its token.

### Authorization

This endpoint requires an authenticated user session with the following
permissions:
- `sanity.{resourceType}.tokens.delete`

info.title: Actions API reference
info.description: Reference documentation for the Actions HTTP endpoint.

servers.description: Parameterized base URL for Sanity API endpoints

projectId.description: Your Sanity project ID. This can be found in your project settings or in the URL when accessing your project in the Sanity management interface.

apiVersion.description: The version of the API to use. This should match the version specified in the info.version field.

BearerAuth.description: Authentication using a Bearer token. The token should be a valid Sanity API token with appropriate permissions for the actions being performed.

Action.description: Base schema for all actions. Every action must specify its type and any required parameters for that specific action type.

actionType.description:
The type of action to perform. This determines what other parameters are required and how the action will be processed.
The actionType must be one of the predefined values that correspond to the available actions in the API.

allOf.description: Creates a new document in the dataset.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document creation action.

publishedId.description: The ID of the published document. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.

ifExists.description: What to do if the document already exists.

document.description: The document to create. Must include `_type` and `_id` fields.

_id.description: The document ID. Note that document["_id"] must have either drafts. or versions.<release>. as a prefix and the portion following that prefix must be equal to publishedId.

allOf.description: Deletes a document from the dataset. The document must exist and you must have permission to delete it.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document deletion action

publishedId.description: The ID of the document to delete. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.

includeDrafts.description: An array of draft or version documents to delete alongside the published document.

purge.description: If true, the document history is also deleted. This cannot be undone.

allOf.description: An edit action is used to modify an existing document. It applies the given patch to the document referenced by versionId. If there is no such document then one is created using the current state of the published version and then that is updated accordingly.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document edit action

publishedId.description: The ID of the document to edit. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.

versionId.description: The ID of the version (or draft) to edit. This should be the full version ID.

patch.description:
The patch to apply to the document. This can include operations like:
- set: Set a field to a value
- unset: Remove a field
- insert: Insert items into an array
- replace: Replace items in an array
- inc: Increment a number
- dec: Decrement a number

See the [patch documentation](https://www.sanity.io/docs/content-lake/http-patches) for more on patches.

allOf.description: Publishes a document, making it available in the published dataset. The document must exist in the draft dataset.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document publish action

publishedId.description: The ID of the document to publish. If the document is an existing draft or version, omit the `drafts.` or `versions.` prefix.

draftId.description:
The ID of the draft to publish. This should be the full draft ID including the 'drafts.' prefix. 
This parameter is deprecated. Use versionId instead.

versionId.description: The ID of the version to publish. This should be the full version ID including the 'versions.' prefix.

ifDraftRevisionId.description: The ID of the draft revision. Used to optimistically lock the draft version to ensure it is not changed during publishing.

ifPublishedRevisionId.description: The ID of the published revision. Used to optimistically lock the published version to ensure it is not changed by other means during publishing.

allOf.description: Unpublishes a document, removing it from the published dataset. The document must exist in the published dataset.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document unpublish action

publishedId.description:
The ID of the document to unpublish. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.
The document must exist in the published dataset.

versionId.description: The ID of the version to unpublish. This should be the full version ID.

allOf.description:
[DEPRECATED] Discards a document. Use version actions instead.
Deletes the draft version of a document. It is an error if it does not exist.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document discard action

versionId.description: The ID of the version to discard. This should be the full version ID.

purge.description: If true, the document history is also deleted. This cannot be undone.

allOf.description:
[DEPRECATED] Replaces a draft document. Use version actions instead.
Replaces the draft version of a document with the provided content.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a document replace draft action

publishedId.description: The ID of the published document to replace the draft with.

purge.description: If true, the document history is also deleted. This cannot be undone.

attributes.description:
The document to replace the draft with. Must include `_id` and `_type` fields.
The `_id` must start with 'drafts.'.

_id.description: The document ID. Must start with 'drafts.'.

_type.description: The document type. Must match a schema type defined in your project.

allOf.description:
Creates a new version of a document in a release. Can either create from a complete document
or copy from an existing document with specified attributes.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a version creation action

publishedId.description: The ID of the published document to create a version of. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.

document.description:
The document to create as a version. Must include `_id` and `_type` fields.
The `_id` must start with 'versions.<release>.' where <release> is the ID of the release.
If not provided, you must specify versionId and baseId to copy from an existing document.

_id.description: The document ID. Must start with 'versions.<release>.' where <release> is the ID of the release.

versionId.description:
The document ID to be created. Required when document is not provided.
Must start with 'versions.<release>.' where <release> is the ID of the release.

baseId.description: The ID of an existing document to copy attributes from. Required when document is not provided.

ifBaseRevisionId.description: Optional revision ID check. The action will fail if this does not match the current revision in the database.

allOf.description: Discards a version of a document. If purge is true, the document history is also deleted.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a version discard action

versionId.description: The ID of the version to discard. This should be the full version ID including the 'versions.<release>.' prefix.

purge.description: If true, the document history is also deleted. This cannot be undone.

allOf.description: Replaces an existing version of a document. The document ID must start with either 'drafts.' or 'versions.<release>.'.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a version replace action

document.description:
The document to replace the existing version with. Must include `_id` and `_type` fields.
The `_id` must start with either 'drafts.' or 'versions.<release>.'.

_id.description: The document ID. Must start with either 'drafts.' or 'versions.<release>.'.

allOf.description: Marks a version for unpublishing. When the release is published, the published document will be removed.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a version unpublish action

versionId.description: The ID of the version to unpublish. This should be the full version ID including the 'versions.<release>.' prefix.

publishedId.description:
The ID of the published document to remove. This should be the full document ID without any prefixes.
The document must exist in the published dataset.

allOf.description: Creates a new release. The releaseId must be unique within your retention limit.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release creation action

releaseId.description:
The unique ID for the release. Must be unique within your retention limit.
Cannot reuse the ID of a previously published or archived release.

metadata.description: Optional metadata for the release. Can include title, description, and scheduling information.

title.description: A human-readable title for the release

description.description: A detailed description of the release

releaseType.description:
The type of release:
- asap: Release will be published as soon as possible
- undecided: Release timing is not yet determined
- scheduled: Release will be published at a specific time

intendedPublishAt.description:
The intended publish time for scheduled releases.
Only used when releaseType is 'scheduled'.

metadata.title: Spring 2024 Release
metadata.description: New features and improvements for spring

allOf.description: Modifies the metadata of an existing release. Only simple JSONMatch expressions starting with 'metadata' are accepted.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release edit action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to edit. The release must exist and not be published.

patch.description:
The patch to apply to the release metadata. Must start with 'metadata'.
Can include operations like set, unset, etc.

metadata.title: Updated Spring 2024 Release
metadata.description: Updated features and improvements for spring

allOf.description: Publishes all documents in a release. For larger releases, the effect may take some time to be fully visible.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release publish action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to publish. The release must exist and not be published.

allOf.description: Archives a release, removing it from active releases. The documents remain in retention and can be accessed via history.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release archive action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to archive. The release must exist and not be published.

allOf.description: Restores an archived release and all its documents to their state before archiving.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release unarchive action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to unarchive. The release must exist and be archived.

allOf.description: Schedules a release for publishing at a future time. The release is locked until the scheduled time.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release schedule action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to schedule. The release must exist and not be published.

publishAt.description: The time to publish the release. Must be in the future.

allOf.description: Cancels a scheduled release. The release is unlocked and can be edited again.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release unschedule action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to unschedule. The release must exist and be scheduled.

allOf.description: Deletes a published or archived release. The backing system document will be removed from the dataset.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release delete action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to delete. The release must exist and be either published or archived.

allOf.description: Imports a release to a dataset. Used by the dataset import CLI to ensure releases are imported into a dataset.

actionType.description: Identifies this as a release import action

releaseId.description: The ID of the release to import.

_id.description: The document ID. Must start with '_.releases.'.

ifExists.description: What to do if the release already exists.

ActionsRequest.description:
A request containing one or more actions to be executed in a single transaction.
All actions must be valid and you must have permission to perform them.

actions.description:
An array of actions to execute. Each action must specify its type and required parameters.
Actions are executed in the order they appear in the array.

dryRun.description: If true, the actions will be executed but the results will not be saved.

skipCrossDatasetReferenceValidation.description: If true then any cross-dataset references will be considered weak for the purposes of this request.

transactionId.description: By default every transaction gets a random ID. This is included in Listener mutation events and the History API. This parameter lets you set your own transaction ID. It must be unique in the dataset.

metadata.title: Spring 2024 Release
metadata.description: New features and improvements for spring

ActionsResponse.description: The response from executing one or more actions. Contains the transaction ID and results for each action.

transactionId.description: The ID of the transaction that was created. This can be used to track the status of the actions.

results.description: The results of each action in the request. Results are in the same order as the actions in the request.

status.description:
The status of the action:
- success: The action was executed successfully
- error: The action failed to execute

error.description: Error information if the action failed. Only present when status is 'error'.

code.description: An error code that can be used to identify the type of error

parameters.name: dataset
parameters.description: The dataset to perform actions on. This is the name of your Sanity dataset.

post.description:
Execute one or more actions in a single transaction. Each action must specify its type and required parameters.
All actions must be valid and you must have permission to perform them.

The actions are executed in the order they appear in the request. If any action fails, the entire transaction is rolled back.

Important notes:
- You cannot mix different types of actions (e.g., release and document actions) in a single API call
- During publishing of larger releases, both source and target documents are locked and cannot be modified
- Release IDs must be unique within your retention limit and cannot be reused for previously published or archived releases
- Documents remain in retention after archiving and can be accessed via the document history endpoint

createDocument.description: Demonstrates how to create a new document of type "post" with a specific ID using the document create action.

deleteDocument.description: Shows how to delete a document by its published ID, including any associated drafts or version documents.

editDocument.description: Demonstrates editing an existing document by applying a patch to a specific version.

publishDocument.description: Publishes a specific version of a document, making it available in the published dataset.

unpublishDocument.description: Unpublishes a document, removing it from the published dataset but retaining its versions and drafts.

createVersionFromDocument.summary: Create a version from a document
createVersionFromDocument.description: Creates a new version of a document in a release by providing the full document object.

createVersionFromExisting.summary: Create a version by copying from an existing document
createVersionFromExisting.description: Creates a new version by copying attributes from an existing document, optionally checking a base revision ID.

discardVersion.description: Discards a specific version of a document, optionally purging its history from the dataset.

replaceVersion.description: Replaces an existing version of a document with a new document object, updating its content.

unpublishVersion.description: Marks a version for unpublishing so that when the release is published, the published document will be removed.

createRelease.description: Demonstrates how to create a new release with metadata, such as a title, using the release create action.

editRelease.description: Shows how to edit the metadata of an existing release using a patch operation.

publishRelease.description: Publishes all documents in a release, making the release content available in the published dataset.

archiveRelease.description: Archives a release, removing it from active releases but retaining its documents in retention.

unarchiveRelease.description: Restores an archived release to its pre-archived state, making it active again.

scheduleRelease.description: Schedules a release to be published at a specific future time, locking the release until then.

unscheduleRelease.description: Cancels a previously scheduled release, unlocking it for further edits.

deleteRelease.description: Deletes a published or archived release from the dataset, removing its backing system document.

multipleActions.summary: Dispatch multiple actions in a single request
multipleActions.description:
Demonstrates how to dispatch multiple actions in a single transaction. In this example, a document is edited and then published in the same request.
Note that it is not permitted to mix different types of actions, such as release and document actions, in a single transaction.

discardDocument.description: [DEPRECATED] Discards a draft version of a document. Use version actions instead.

replaceDraftDocument.description: [DEPRECATED] Replaces a draft document with new content. Use version actions instead.

200.description: The actions were successfully executed. The response includes the transaction ID and results for each action.

400.description:
The request was invalid. This could be due to:
- Missing required fields
- Invalid action types
- Invalid parameters for actions
- Invalid document IDs or types
- Mixing different types of actions in a single request
- Attempting to reuse a release ID from a published or archived release
- Attempting to modify locked documents during a publish operation

401.description: The request was not authenticated. Make sure you're using a valid API token.

403.description:
The request was not authorized. Your API token doesn't have permission to perform the requested actions.
This could also occur if you attempt to modify documents that are locked during a publish operation.

409.description:
The request conflicts with the current state of the system. This could be due to:
- Attempting to modify documents that are locked during a publish operation
- Attempting to unschedule a release that is referenced by a later scheduled release
- Attempting to reuse a release ID that is still within the retention period

500.description:
An internal server error occurred. This is usually temporary and the request can be retried.
For larger releases, publishing operations may take some time to complete, during which the documents
will be locked and cannot be modified.

post.description:
Prompt the LLM for a response. Does not interact with documents beyond fetching content as context when requested.
Use for tasks that don't require manipulating documents, such as generating details that you may want to use with other Agent Actions.

post.summary: Make a schema-aware patch to a document
post.description:
A schema-aware patch API that validates the provided paths and values against the target schema.
This operation does not use an LLM and will only use regular Sanity billing for API requests.

Path.title: Path
Path.description:
Defines the starting point, or root, of the area to affect.

To further control the behavior, use `include`, `exclude`, `types.include`, and `types.exclude`.

Examples:
```json
"body"  // Simple string path
{ "_key": "section1" }  // Array item by key
```

PathList.title: Path List
PathList.description:
A list of paths that define the target area.
Each path in the list can be either a string or a key object.

Operation.title: Operation Type
Operation.description:
Sets the default operation for all paths in the target.

Operation types:
  - 'set': Overwrites and replaces the full field value.
  - 'append':
    - array fields: Appends new items to the end of the array.
    - string fields: Appends new content to the end of the string.
    - text fields: Appends new content to a new line after the existing content.
    - number fields: The sum of the new value and the existing value.
    - Other fields types not mentioned will use `set` instead.
  - 'mixed': Sets non-array fields, and appends array fields.

Use `include` to override the default `operation` for specific paths.

Nested fields inherit the operation of their parent, and fall back
to the top level target operation unless otherwise specified.

PatchOperation.title: Patch Operation Type
PatchOperation.description:
Determines how the target path will be patched.
- `'set'`: an *overwriting* operation: sets the full field value for primitive targets, and merges the provided value with existing values for objects
- `'append'`:
    - array fields: appends new items to the end of the array,
    - string fields: '"existing content" "new content"'
    - text fields: '"existing content"\\n"new content"'
    - number fields: existing + new
    - other field types not mentioned will set instead (dates, url)
- `'mixed'`:  sets non-array fields, and appends to array fields
- `'unset'`: removes whatever value is on the target path

All operations except unset requires a `value`.

#### Appending in the middle of arrays
To append to an array, use 'append' the operation and provide an array value with one or more array items.
`target: {path: ['array'], operation: 'append', value: [{_type: 'item' _key: 'a'}]}` will append the items in the value to the existing array.
To insert in the middle of the array, use `target: {path: ['array', {_key: 'appendAfterKey'}], operation: 'append', value: [{_type: 'item' _key: 'a'}]}`.
Here, `{_type: 'item' _key: 'a'}` will be appended after the array item with key `'appendAfterKey'`
It is optional to provide a _key for inserted array items; if non is not provided, it will be generated.

TypeConfig.title: Type Configuration
TypeConfig.description:
Configure which document types to include or exclude from the instruction.

Example:
```json
{
  "include": ["text", "image", "video"]
}
```

maxPathDepth.description:
The maximum depth of the document paths the instruction can write to.
Depth is based on field path segments:
- `title` has a depth of 1
- `author.name` has a depth of 2
- `array[_key="123"].title` has a depth of 3

Use caution when setting this value too high in studios with recursive document schemas
as it may cause performance and response quality issues.

Target.title: Target Configuration

GenerateTarget.title: Generate Target Configuration
GenerateTarget.description: Defines which parts of the document will be affected by the Generate instruction.

Include.title: Include Configuration

Exclude.title: Exclude Configuration
Exclude.description:
Define fields or array items to exclude from the instruction. Any omitted fields or array items are implicitly included.
By default, all children up to the maxPathDepth are included.

Example:
```json
["metadata", "draft", { "_key": "tempSection" }]
```

TransformOperation.title: Transform Operation Type
TransformOperation.description:
Sets the default operation for all paths in the target.

Operation types:
  - 'set': Overwrites and replaces the full field value.
  - 'append':
    - array fields: Appends new items to the end of the array.
    - string fields: Appends new content to the end of the string.
    - text fields: Appends new content to a new line after the existing content.
    - number fields: The sum of the new value and the existing value.
    - Other fields types not mentioned will use `set` instead.
  - 'mixed': Sets non-array fields, and appends array fields.
  - 'image-description': Lets the transform action select an image asset, select a field, and describe the image in the field. You can also set an `imageUrl` to describe remote images.

Use `include` to override the default `operation` for specific paths.

Nested fields inherit the operation of their parent, and fall back
to the top level target operation unless otherwise specified.

type.description: Use to describe an image asset. It will find an adjacent asset if located in the image path. For external paths, use sourcePath. For remote images, use imageUrl.

imageUrl.description: The URL of the image to describe.

sourcePath.description: The path to the image asset to describe.

The target document to transform and the operation to perform. 
If omitted, the source document (`documentId`) will be used as the target.

oneOf.title: Edit Document

schemaId.description: The schema identifier reported by `sanity deploy` or `sanity schema store`.

conditionalPaths.description: Allows the instruction to modify conditional `readOnly` or `hidden` fields. Does not apply to fields explicitly set as hidden or readOnly in the schema.

defaultReadOnly.description: When false, forces all conditional readOnly fields to be writable.

defaultHidden.description: When false, forces all conditional hidden fields to be writable.

paths.description: Override the default behavior of individual conditional paths.

path.description: Must be the full document path, regardless of the path defined in `target`.

readOnly.description: When false, the field is writable.

hidden.description: When false, the field is writable.

forcePublishedWrite.description:
Set to true to mutate published documents. By default, Agent Actions will never mutate a published document. They will use a draftId if a publishedId is provided.
Operations on drafts receive initial state from the published document, if no draft exists. If a draft exists, it is used as the initial state.
The returned `_id` will be that of the modified document.

Documents set to `liveEdit: true` will be mutated as if `forcePublishedWrite: true`.

temperature.description: Controls the level of variance in the response. A higher value returns more variation in responses, while a lower value returns more predictable responses.

localeSettings.description:
When provided with locale settings, Generate will write to `date` and `datetime` fields.
Otherwise, those fields will be ignored.

locale.description: A valid unicode [BCP 47 locale identifier](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl#getcanonicalocales). This will affect how natural language time and date phrases are parsed and presented.

Parameter.title: Instruction Parameter
Parameter.description:
Parameter values available to reference in the instruction.

There are four types of parameters:
- `constant`: Set a static value
- `field`: Target a field in the document to set the parameter to its value
- `groq`: Set the parameter to the result of a GROQ query
- `document`: Set the parameter to the value of the entire document

FieldParam.title: Field Parameter
FieldParam.description:
Set the parameter to the value of the field at the given path.
If no `documentId` is provided, the current document is used. Note: for prompt request, the documentId is required.

styleGuide.example: Use a formal tone and avoid using contractions.

A list of phrases that should not be translated. 
Use this instead of the style guide for phrases or words you want the translation to leave unchanged.

languageFieldPath.description:
Set this to the path of the field where the language code is set in your document schema.
This will ensure the new document has the correct language code added after translation. 
This has no affect on the translation process.

documentId.description: The ID of the source document to transform.

Set to the document ID of the document you want to modify. 
This is the shorthand equivalent of `targetDocument: { _id: '123', operation: 'edit' }`.
Mutually exclusive with `targetDocument`.

Instruction.title: Instruction Text
Instruction.description: Describes the instruction. Reference `instructionParams` using the `$variable` syntax.
Instruction.example: Create a new document with a title like $title, that matches the style of $externalTitle, and generate content based on the $backgroundContext.

InstructionParams.title: Instruction Parameters
InstructionParams.description:
Parameter values available to reference in the instruction.

There are four types of parameters:
- `constant`: Set a static value. Ex: `paramName: { type: 'constant', value: 'New title'}`
- `field`: Target a field in the document to set the parameter to its value. Also takes an optional `documentId` to target a field in a different document. Ex: `paramName: { type: 'field', path: 'poster'}`
- `groq`: Set the parameter to the result of a GROQ query. Ex: `paramName: { type: 'groq', query: '*[_type == $docType]', params: { 'docType': 'article'} }`
- `document`: Set the parameter to the value of the entire document. If no `documentID` is provided, the current document is returned. Ex: `paramName: { type: 'document', documentId: '123'}`

IncludeInstruction.title: Include Instruction Text
IncludeInstruction.description: Describes the instructions for the included targets. References the top-level `instructionParams` using the `$variable` syntax.

TransformTarget.title: Transform Target Configuration
TransformTarget.description: Defines which parts of the document will be affected by the Transform instruction.

TransformInclude.title: Transform Include
TransformInclude.description: Defines which parts of the document will be included in the Transform instruction.

InitialValues.title: Initial Values
InitialValues.description: Define any initial values for the document. Similar to initial value templates in Studio, this is useful for setting fields to pre-determined values directly without going through the LLM.

The Copy API, also known as Cloud Clone, allows you to make a copy of an existing dataset.
It offers an alternative to exporting and importing a dataset. 
This feature is also [available in the CLI](https://www.sanity.io/docs/how-to-use-cloud-clone-for-datasets). 
All requests to this endpoint must be [authenticated](https://www.sanity.io/docs/http-auth).

### Check the status of the copy operation
You can check the status of the copy operation by making a request to the [Jobs API](https://www.sanity.io/docs/jobs-api) with the returned jobId.

### Paid feature
This feature is part of our Advanced Dataset Management offering on the enterprise plan. Contact us if you need this feature and want to discuss this plan.

info.title: Export API reference
info.description: Reference documentation for the export HTTP endpoint.

get.description:
The Export API allows you to export all the non-deleted documents in a dataset, including drafts and asset documents.

By default, this endpoint returns all the documents a user can access as an NDJSON stream. The documents will be in the state they were when the request was made and will not be affected by any changes in the dataset that happened after the request was made.
In most cases, you probably want to use the CLI tool's export functionality or the JavaScript npm package. Those have built-in features such as asset downloads and an option for including drafts.

### Cursor mode for large datasets
For datasets with many documents (typically tens of thousands), you can experience that network or other instabilities cause the export stream to time out or be interrupted. In these cases, you can send a request a nextCursor query parameter set to an empty string. This will cause the export to run for 30 seconds and end with an object with a nextCursor key and value. The cursor is an encoded representation of a position in an ordered document list of the dataset.

You can initiate a new export request with this value as the nextQuery parameter value to continue the export manually. Note that any changes made between requests in "cursor mode" will be reflected in the export, which can lead to inconsistencies, for example, if references have been changed in the dataset.

Tip: An unauthenticated request will only return publicly available documents. If you include an authentication token, it will consist of documents that this token can access (for example, drafts and system documents).

schema.description: A comma-separated list of document types to include in the export.

The cursor to continue the export from. Set to an empty string to start a new export in "cursor mode".

Cursor mode inserts a `{"nextCursor": "..."}` object into the stream.

parameters.name: autoGenerateArrayKeys
parameters.description: If true, adds a _key attribute to array items, unique within the array, to ensure each can be addressed uniquely in a real-time collaboration context, even if elements are inserted or removed elsewhere in the array.

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: By default every transaction gets a random ID. This is included in Listener mutation events and the History API. This parameter lets you set your own transaction ID. It must be unique in the dataset.

parameters.name: skipCrossDatasetReferencesValidation
parameters.description: If true then any cross-dataset references will be considered weak for the purposes of this mutation.

parameters.name: visibility
parameters.description: Can be: sync, async or deferred If sync the request will not return until the requested changes are visible to subsequent queries, if async the request will return immediately when the changes have been committed, but it might still be a second or so until you can see the change reflected in a query. For maximum performance, use async always, except when you need your next query to see the changes you made. deferred is the fastest way to write. It bypasses the real time indexing completely, and should be used in cases where you are bulk importing/mutating a large number of documents and don't need to see that data in a query for several tens of seconds.

parameters.name: dryRun
parameters.description: If true, the mutation will be validated but not executed

documentId.description: The ID of the affected document

CreateMutation.description:
Creates a new document. The rules for the new document's identifier are:
- If _id is missing, a new random unique ID is generated
- If _id ends with '.', it is used as a prefix for a new random unique ID
- If _id is present, it is used as-is

The operation will fail if a document by the provided ID already exists.

_id.description:
Document ID. Can be:
- Omitted to generate a random ID
- A string ending with '.' to use as prefix for random ID
- A specific ID to use as-is

_createdAt.description: Creation timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

_updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

CreateOrReplaceMutation.description:
Creates a new document or replaces an existing one. If the document already exists:
- If the type is the same, the document will be completely replaced
- If the type is different, it will act as a delete then create
- If the document has hard references pointing to it, changing its type is not allowed

_createdAt.description: Creation timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

_updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

CreateIfNotExistsMutation.description:
Creates a new document, but will silently fail if the document already exists.
Otherwise identical to create mutation.

createIfNotExists.description: Document to create if it doesn't exist

_createdAt.description: Creation timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

_updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp. If omitted, current time is used

DeleteMutation.description:
Deletes a document. Can delete by ID or by GROQ query.
The operation is considered successful even if the document did not exist.

When using a query to delete multiple documents:
- The query can only operate on up to 10,000 documents
- For larger sets, split into multiple transactions
- Recommended to paginate by _id using queries like '*[_type == "article" && _id > $lastId]'
- Deleting system documents requires "manage" permission.

query.description:
GROQ query to select documents to delete.
Example: '*[_type == "feature" && viewCount < $views]'

ifRevisionID.description:
Revision ID for optimistic locking.
If the document's current revision ID doesn't match,
the delete will fail. This can be used for optimistic locking, to make sure that mutations are applied in the exact order expected by the application.

purge.description:
If true, fully purges the document from transaction history.
All transactions related to the document will be immediately removed,
consistent with data retention policy.

PatchMutation.description:
Updates an existing document's contents. A patch will fail if the document does not exist.
Can patch by ID or by GROQ query.

If multiple patches are included, the order of execution is:
1. set
2. setIfMissing
3. unset
4. inc
5. dec
6. insert

query.description:
GROQ query to select documents to patch.
Example: '*[_type == "person" && points >= $threshold]'

ifRevisionID.description:
Revision ID for optimistic locking.
If the document's current revision ID doesn't match,
the patch will fail. This can be used for optimistic locking, to make sure that mutations are applied in the exact order expected by the application.

setIfMissing.description: Fields to set if they don't exist

get.description:
The basic query API endpoint. Due to limitations in some browsers, we've put the max limit on GET queries at 11 KB. 
If your URL is longer than that, you must submit the query as a POST.

Note: When a document is not found, GROQ still evaluates to a value. Thus, you don't get 404 errors through this endpoint.
Some examples:
* `*[_id == "missing"]` results in `[]`
* `count(*[_type == "typoType"])` results in `0`
* `*[_id == "missing"][0]` results in `null`
* `*[_id == "missing"][0].someProp` also results in null

parameters.name: resultSourceMap
parameters.description: If true, the query result will include content source map metadata

parameters.name: perspective
parameters.description:
Runs the query against the selected perspective:
* `drafts`: Query runs as if all draft documents and changes were published.
* `published`: Query runs as if no draft documents or changes exist.
* `raw`: Query runs without distinguishing between drafts, release versions, or published documents.
* Release stack: You can also pass a comma-separated list of release names from Content Releases to create a custom perspective

post.description:

Queries sent using the POST method will also be cached on the CDN.

params.description: The params-part is optional. You don't use the $-prefixes when submitting as json

info.title: Webhooks API reference
info.description: Reference documentation for the Webhooks HTTP endpoints.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the webhook was created

isDisabled.description: Whether the webhook is currently disabled

deletedAt.description: Timestamp when the webhook was deleted, if applicable

url.description: The URL field is where you specify the endpoint to which the webhook request is sent

dataset.description: Which dataset should this trigger for. '*' means all datasets in the project

description.description: Optional description to add helpful context to the webhook

rule.description: Configuration for when and how the webhook should trigger

on.description: Webhooks can be triggered when a document is created, updated, deleted, or any combination of these

filter.description: A GROQ filter specifying which documents will, when changed, trigger your webhook. A filter is what you commonly see between the `*[` and `]` in a GROQ query. This field supports all the GROQ functions you'd expect and has additional support for functions in the delta:: namespace, as well as before() and after(). If left empty, it will apply to all documents (`*[]`)

projection.description: A GROQ projection defining the payload (or body) of the outgoing webhook request. This field supports GROQ functions in the delta:: namespace, as well as before() and after(). If left empty, it will include the whole document after the change that triggered it

apiVersion.description: Which API version to use for the GROQ filter and projection.

httpMethod.description: This field configures the webhook's HTTP request method. It can be set to POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, or GET. Some endpoints require incoming requests to use a specific method to work

includeDrafts.description: Set to trigger on changes to draft documents.

includeAllVersions.description: Set to trigger on changes to version documents.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers. You can add multiple headers. A common example is adding an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header to authenticate the webhook request

secret.description: To let receiving services verify the origin of any outgoing webhook, you may add a secret that will be hashed and included as part of the webhook request's headers

isDisabledByUser.description: Set to 'true' to disable the webhook and prevent it from triggering
